message: Foundations: agent planning, Kotlin/Multiplatform, native CLI, property graph

summary: An ADR-like decision graph that lets coding agents propose architecture changes during the plan stage.

title: Agents propose architecture during planning
summary: The tool must let an agent read, propose, and revise architecture decisions as part of the plan stage.

title: Use Clikt for the command surface
summary: A scriptable subcommand CLI is the primary interface for agents and humans.

title: Target Kotlin/Multiplatform (JVM + native)
summary: One codebase compiled for the JVM and for native macOS/Linux targets.

title: Kotlin is the implementation language
summary: The project is written in Kotlin, so tooling and serialization should be Kotlin-native.

title: Model architecture as a property graph
summary: Decisions, constraints, questions, options, and outcomes are nodes; edges carry first-class rationale.

title: Ship as a self-contained native CLI
summary: Users run a single native executable with no JVM runtime required.

rationale: Kotlin/Native compiles to a standalone executable with no JVM runtime for users.
A fixed Kotlin codebase makes Kotlin/Multiplatform the natural way to reach every target.
Agents drive the tool programmatically, so a scriptable command surface is required.
A queryable graph lets an agent reason about architecture the way it reasons about code.

message: Append-only JSONL change-set log, review workflow, HTTP API, HTML review; drop Cosmograph
author: codex

title: Append-only JSONL log as source of truth
summary: Every line is a record; the graph is never edited in place, only appended.

title: Reviewable change-sets (proposed / accepted / rejected)
summary: Records sharing a change_id form one unit of design; the current graph is the projection of accepted change-sets.

title: Serve an editable graph and review API over HTTP
summary: Ktor CIO exposes /api/graph, /api/log, and accept/reject endpoints.

title: Interactive HTML with change timeline and authoring
summary: A self-contained page renders the graph plus a timeline of change-sets for human review.

title: Import legacy single-file JSON graphs
summary: A migration path folds an old single-file graph into a new JSONL log.

title: Keep one SVG renderer; drop the WebGL engine
summary: Remove the Cosmograph engine to avoid maintaining two render paths.

title: Single mutable JSON file
summary: Store the whole graph in one file edited in place.

title: WebGL Cosmograph renderer
summary: A second, GPU-based rendering engine for very large graphs.

rationale: Reviewable units let an agent propose changes a human can approve or reject.
An append-only history makes every proposal an auditable, revertible unit.
Reviewers need a live surface to accept or reject proposals.
The timeline visualizes proposed versus accepted change-sets for human review.
The property graph is exactly what the HTML view renders and navigates.
A single mutable file cannot represent reviewable, append-only history.
A second WebGL engine duplicated the renderer with little added value.

message: Markdown render engine, force-directed SVG layout, and agent skills
author: claude

title: Markdown render format for agents
summary: Render any projection to agent-readable Markdown so an agent reads the architecture before proposing.

title: Force-directed SVG layout
summary: Constant-size nodes, connectivity-ordered seed, and grid-accelerated repulsion so connected nodes sit close and the field stays coarse.

title: Ship keemun skills for Claude Code and Codex
summary: A keemun usage skill plus a run-keemun build skill teach agents the review/propose/apply loop.

rationale: An agent reads a proposed change-set as Markdown before deciding what to do.
Markdown is a projection of the same property graph, shaped for agent reading.
The force layout is the SVG the human-review HTML draws.
The skill tells agents to read the Markdown view first.
The skill routes the HTML view to the human for approval.
Skills encode the plan-stage workflow agents follow.
